narrative: OMX30 opens to conflicting signals: global risk appetite and strong US markets support a bullish setup, while technical deterioration and rising volatility (VIX +3.05%) introduce caution. The index sits +5.8% above its 200-MA but only +0.6% above 50-MA, with MACD rolling over and strong bearish ADX divergence signaling potential mean reversion. Brent crude at +4.07% is a headwind for industrials (28% of index), while rising yields (+65bps) help banks but compress tech valuations. The 52-week positioning at 88% of range leaves limited upside cushion.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a classic bull-trap setup: transatlantic equity strength and USD strength should be constructive, but technicals are rolling over hard. MACD below signal, ADX showing strong bearish bias despite 49.8 reading, and RSI at 40 mid-range suggest consolidation or modest pullback before any fresh rally. Oil at +4.07% pressures Volvo/Atlas Copco/industrials despite USD/SEK tailwind; banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) get a modest boost from 465bp yields. Watch 3123 pivot closely—break below 3100 risks test of lower Bollinger (~3085). Key level: 3180 (50-MA) caps upside. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could reignite volatility. Copper flat; Boliden gets only marginal help from gold +1.07%.
drivers: US equity strength (+0.89% S&P, +1.29% NASDAQ) supports risk appetite
USD/SEK strength (+0.34%) benefits exporters but oil spike (+4.07%) pressures industrials
Rising US yields (+0.65% to 4.628%) widen bank NIMs but compress tech DCF valuations
Strong ADX 49.8 with bearish DI bias signals directional weakness despite bull market regime
RSI 40.21 neutral but MACD below signal line warns momentum deterioration

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +65bps to 4.628% widens NIM expectations; Swedish rates likely to follow, supporting deposit margins.
Brent crude +4.07% to 94.71 raises input costs and energy expense. USD/SEK strength (+0.34%) partially offsets but not enough to overcome commodity headwind.
NASDAQ +1.29% is supportive, but rising US yields compress DCF valuations. MACD deterioration and RSI neutral-low suggest momentum headwinds.
Gold +1.07% and copper flat (−0.05%) provide marginal support; insufficient to drive meaningful outperformance.
Oil spike and consumer spending pressure offset by EUR/SEK weakness (−0.30% favours sourcing). Mixed backdrop.
Rising yields pressure refinancing and cap rates. No relief from equity strength.

watchList.reason: Yield curve steepening (US 10Y +65bps) expands NIM; strong technical level support at 3123 pivotal for index.
USD/SEK strength is offset by crude oil spike (+4.07%); input cost pressure likely dominates. Industrials facing headwind.
NASDAQ strength neutral-positive but MACD deterioration and tech yield compression create offsetting pressures. Await technical clarity.
Gold +1.07% supportive but copper flat; insufficient catalyst for significant move.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish macro releases today. US Jobless Claims (initial jobless claims) at 14:30 CET—key data for Fed narrative. Watch for any surprise that could swing USD/SEK and equity sentiment.
topRisk: US Jobless Claims surprise (either direction) could spike VIX further and trigger momentum capitulation given MACD rollover. Rising oil (geopolitical tail risk) could accelerate industrials weakness if it breaks above 95.
technicalSummary: Bull market regime: +5.8% above 200-MA (strong uptrend intact)
MACD below signal line with negative histogram (−6.731) — key warning
ADX 49.8 strong trend but bearish DI bias — divergence suggests reversal risk
RSI 40.21 neutral midrange — not oversold, no mean-reversion bounce signal yet
Bollinger %B=0.37 in lower half — room to compress further
52-week at 88% range, −2.7% from high — limited upside, downside cushion improving
Stochastic K=30.8 D=21.4 — both in neutral zone, no directional conviction
